title: TC001-Successful login redirects to Dashboard
description: Verifies a user can log in with valid credentials and is redirected to the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:5175/login) and wait for the page to load so the login form elements become available.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided email into email input (index 155), type provided password into password input (index 159), then click the 'Entrar' button (index 166).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrojuliooberlaender@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pedro30102002!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type credentials into the current fields (email index 192, password index 193) and click 'Entrar' (button index 196) to attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrojuliooberlaender@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pedro30102002!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Login').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Invalid password shows error and stays on Login
description: Verifies an incorrect password displays an invalid-credentials error and does not navigate away from the login page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page at /login (http://localhost:5175/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Credenciais inválidas').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login form not rendered: the page contained no interactive elements (email/password inputs and submit button not found).
- Email input field not found on the /login page, so credentials could not be entered.
- Password input field not found on the /login page, so the login attempt could not be performed.
- 'Entrar' button not found on the /login page, preventing submission and error-triggering.
- Could not verify error text 'Credenciais inválidas' or confirm the URL remained on /login because the form could not be submitted.

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

            ],

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Submit button not interactable: multiple click attempts on the login submit button failed due to the element being not interactable or stale.
- Page rendering instability: after interactions the SPA intermittently renders a blank page showing 0 interactive elements, preventing progression.
- Login submission never completed, so the dashboard was not reached and session persistence could not be verified.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Navigate to /login by issuing a navigate action to http://localhost:5175/login so the login page can be loaded.

        # -> Input the username into the email field (index 82).

        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrojuliooberlaender@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pedro30102002!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        # -> Type credentials into the email and password fields and click the Entrar button to log in (inputs: email index=169, password index=173, button index=180). Proceed to click refresh after successful login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrojuliooberlaender@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pedro30102002!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=KPIs').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Conversion Chart').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Recent Leads').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC013-Automatic Assistant: search, paginate, and open WhatsApp chat from a lead row

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:5175/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username into the email input (index 82) then type the password into the password input (index 86) and submit by clicking the Entrar button (index 93).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrojuliooberlaender@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pedro30102002!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)

        # --> Assertions to verify final state
